summary_one_line: Font must carry a structurally valid kern table that reaches has_machine_kerning returning false, not all-0x20 padding.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font blob whose kern-table region (offsets ~0x10-0x2f) holds concrete non-padding sub-table offset/length fields rather than 0x20 filler, so kerning-table parsing advances far enough to evaluate has_machine_kerning and return false.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry varied structural bytes at the table region (e.g. 0x10=00, 0x18=01/35, 0x1a=00/35) instead of uniform 0x20; value_profile's CMP_MAP distance feedback rewards inputs whose parsed table-length/offset comparisons move closer to satisfying the kern-table validity checks, preserving partial progress as corpus.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser (naive) seeds are dominated by 0x20 padding across 0x06-0x2f, so the kern sub-table fields never form a valid offset chain; naive has no distance signal to incrementally close the gap and stays blocked on the true side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input whose kern-parse comparisons get closer to passing adds a new bucket and is kept, giving a gradient toward the structurally-valid font that naive lacks.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font that reaches the kern-table path through the same edges naive hits but under a call context that registers as new coverage, sustaining corpus entries that drive deeper into hb_ot_shape_planner_t::compile toward the blocker.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain (compile <- init0 <- create2 ...) into the edge-map index, so edges in the kern-decision block at L168-L187 are recorded as new (call-context, edge) pairs, keeping more intermediate font states alive and exploring the false side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive uses bare edge buckets, so once the L185-L187 edges are hit once under any context they no longer yield new coverage; the loser's 0x20-padded inputs never accrue the structural variety needed and it remains blocked (resolved 2/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook indexes edges by call context, turning the same kern-decision edge into multiple distinct coverage slots and thereby preserving inputs that incrementally build the valid kern table that flips the branch false.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A compact, fast-executing font seed whose kern-table fields are concrete enough to reach the has_machine_kerning evaluation; the calibrated power schedule then spends extra mutation energy on such seeds to complete the valid structure.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer's winning seeds are small (44-66 bytes) with concrete table bytes (e.g. F2020 28f6 6b65726e 'kern' header at 0x06-0x0f); calibrated energy gives faster/smaller/stabler seeds more havoc mutations per visit, concentrating effort on the near-valid font and reaching the false side fastest (avg blocked 2.90h vs loser 15.40h).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive runs a plain mutational stage with uniform energy, so it dilutes effort across many 0x20-padded seeds that never form a valid kern table; it stays blocked 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage derives per-seed energy from a calibration perf_score (no rarity weighting), so small fast near-valid font seeds earn disproportionate havoc mutations that complete the kern-table structure flipping the branch false.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a structural kerning-table predicate, not a magic-number comparison gate.
Three distinct techniques each beat naive at this branch, so divergence is multi-axis.
ctx_coverage is the delta for the naive_ctx pair.
calibrated_energy is the delta for the minimizer pair.
Loser-blocking seeds are dominated by 0x20 padding in the table region.
Winner-resolving seeds carry concrete non-padding structural bytes in the table region.
The byte diff shows winners diverge from losers' uniform 0x20 at structural offsets like 0x18-0x1a.
value_profile rewards CMP-operand pairs that get closer to matching by adding a new CMP_MAP bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx folds call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: The kern-decision edges at L168-L187 are on the call chain to the blocker and show W/L divergence.
minimizer derives per-seed mutation energy from calibration so faster, smaller, more-stable seeds earn more mutations.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer reaches the resolving side fastest, with the lowest blocked duration of the three winners.
The W role takes the false (winning) branch side at L187 while the L role never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 187  T=0 F=40  T=14 F=0  || !hb_ot_layout_has_machine_kerning (face)  <-- BLOCKER

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Not an I2SRandReplace claim, so no lineage query is mandatory. The decisive deltas are value_profile (CMP_MAP gradient), ctx_coverage, and calibrated_energy — none is cmplog/vpc I2S. The primary hypothesis (Pair 1) attributes the winner to value_profile's CMP_MAP distance feedback; MECHANISM CONTEXT confirms value_profile uses no I2SRandReplace and only havoc/token mutators, so the dash-row floor check does not apply here.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the kern-table region is replaced with uniform 0x20 padding still resolves the false branch under naive as often as under value_profile/naive_ctx/minimizer, showing the structural-validity feature is not what gates the branch.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether value_profile's win is specifically CMP_MAP distance feedback on kern-table length comparisons rather than incidental extra corpus diversity from CMP buckets is not directly observable in the prompt.
